content.problemStatement:
Sereja has an array consisting of nnn integers a1 ≤a2 ≤…≤ana_{1}  \leq a_{2}  \leq \ldots \leq a_{n}a1​ ≤a2​ ≤…≤an​. Based on this array, he has to answer mmm queries represented by pairs integers ttt and ddd.

The answer for a query is the smallest integer iii for which there exist some kkk (i ≤ ki  \leq  ki ≤ k) such that ai + d ≥ ai + 1a_{i} + d  \geq  a_{i + 1}ai​ + d ≥ ai + 1​, ai + 1 + d ≥ ai + 2a_{i + 1} + d  \geq  a_{i + 2}ai + 1​ + d ≥ ai + 2​, …\ldots…, ak − 1 + d≥ aka_{k - 1}  +  d \geq  a_{k}ak − 1​ + d≥ ak​, ak ≤ ta_{k}  \leq  tak​ ≤ t and ak+1 > ta_{k+1} > tak+1​ > t (if it exists).

Help Sereja find the answer for each query.
content.inputFormat: First line contains a single integer n, representing the number of elements in the array. The second line contains n space-separated integers, representing the array elements. The third line contains a single integer m, representing the number of queries. The next m lines contain two space-separated integers, representing the query parameters t and d.
content.outputFormat: For each query, output the smallest integer i for which there exists some k (i ≤ k) such that ai + d ≥ ai+1, ai+1 + d ≥ ai+2, …, ak-1 + d ≥ ak, ak ≤ t, and ak+1 > t (if it exists). If no such i exists, output -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For each query, we find the smallest i that satisfies the conditions.
For each query, we find the smallest i that satisfies the conditions.
For each query, we find the smallest i that satisfies the conditions.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over the array and checking for each element if it satisfies the given conditions. We can use two pointers to keep track of the current element and the next element.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n*m) per test case, O(n*m) total. Precomputation is not required.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) — the dominant structure is the input array.
